chart.title: Per capita electricity generation by source
chart.subtitle: Measured in kilowatt-hours. "Other renewables" include geothermal, tidal, and wave generation.
chart.citation: Ember (2026); Energy Institute - Statistical Review of World Energy (2025); Population based on various sources (2024)

Coal electricity per capita - kWh (adapt.titleShort: Electricity generation from coal per person
Coal electricity per capita - kWh (adapt.titleLong: Electricity generation from coal per person
descriptionKey: Per capita figures are calculated by dividing total values by the population of the country or region. Population data is constructed by Our World in Data, based on [various sources](https://ourworldindata.org/grapher/population).
Coal electricity per capita - kWh (adapt.descriptionProcessing: - Electricity data from 2000 onwards (and from 1990 onwards for European countries, including Turkey) comes from Ember. Earlier data comes from the Energy Institute.
Coal electricity per capita - kWh (adapt.citationShort: Ember (2026); Energy Institute - Statistical Review of World Energy (2025); Population based on various sources (2024) – with major processing by Our World in Data
Coal electricity per capita - kWh (adapt.citationLong: Ember (2026); Energy Institute - Statistical Review of World Energy (2025); Population based on various sources (2024) – with major processing by Our World in Data. “Electricity generation from coal per person” [dataset]. Ember, “Yearly Electricity Data Europe”; Ember, “Yearly Electricity Data”; Energy Institute, “Statistical Review of World Energy”; Various sources, “Population” [original data].
